findings.title: WMA(3) has no gaze-gap guard â€” blends seconds-old samples after blinks/tracking loss, painting ghost cursor hops
findings.scenario: Patient is reading at point A on a web page, blinks (or tracking drops while glancing at a caregiver) and re-opens looking at point B on the other side of the page. During the gap no frames arrive, so wmaPrev1Ref/wmaPrev2Ref still hold A. The first post-gap frame forwards 0.45Â·B + 0.55Â·A and the second forwards 0.75Â·B + 0.25Â·A â€” both pass the >18px snap gate, so the in-page cursor visibly hops A â†’ 45%-point â†’ 75%-point â†’ B over ~30ms instead of jumping straight to B as it did before this commit. Purely a transient visual artifact: no dwell misfire is reachable (a 1-2 frame touch cannot reach onsetMsÂ·0.6 = 168ms, page-side progress save needs â‰¥5%, click needs 1100ms), but the patient sees the cursor flash at wrong intermediate positions after every blink-with-movement.
findings.evidence: forward() is event-driven per gaze frame (ws.subscribeGaze(() => forward()) at line 3854), so during a blink no calls occur and the WMA refs are never aged or invalidated â€” there is no timestamp on them. The blend `gazeNow.x * 0.45 + w1.x * 0.30 + w2.x * 0.25` therefore treats a sample from arbitrarily long ago as 'previous frame'. This is inconsistent with the rest of this same patch series, which explicitly defines >150ms frame gaps as discontinuities (commit d9fb816 gapPauseMs on the page side, and docs/EYE_TRACKING_CHANGES.md Entry 8). The commit message claims 'Filter state resets whenever the cursor hides or the browser session reopens' â€” true for the three hide paths (lines 3766-68, 3784-85, 3801-02 all null both refs), but the cursor does NOT hide during a stream gap, so the stale pair survives. Pre-commit behavior: `raw = gpRef.current` directly, so the first post-gap frame snapped to B in one frame with no intermediate points. Fix shape: record lastForwardAt and null both WMA refs when (nowMs - lastForwardAt) > ~150ms, mirroring gapPauseMs.

findings.title: WMA in front of the >18px snap gate raises the effective single-frame snap threshold to ~40px; in-file comment now wrong
findings.scenario: Patient shifts fixation between adjacent dense controls 18-40px apart (e.g., the YouTube control row, or consecutive links one text line apart). The backend fixation filter emits this as a near single-frame step of D px. Before this commit the page cursor snapped to the new control in one frame (D > 18 triggered the snap branch). Now the gate sees only 0.45Â·D on the first frame, so for D â‰¤ 40px the snap never fires and the cursor instead glides via the alpha-0.9 EMA: frame 1 lands at 40.5% of the way, frame 2 at ~71.6%, frame 3 at ~97%, then the <1.5px jitter gate freezes the sub-2px residual. Net patient effect: cursor settle on the new control is ~30-45ms slower and for one frame the cursor paints between the two buttons; page-side dwell onset on the new target starts correspondingly late. No dwell-timing constant changes and no misclick path exists (click requires the full 1100ms dwell) â€” bounded latency regression, not a crawl.
findings.evidence: After a step Aâ†’B: frame 1 raw = 0.45B+0.55A (w1=w2=A), so dist = |raw âˆ’ prev(A)| = 0.45Â·D â€” snap branch `if (dist > 18)` (line 3829) requires D > 40px instead of D > 18px. Convergence trace for D=40 with alpha 0.9 (line 3842): residuals 23.8 â†’ 11.4 â†’ 1.14px (then jitter-held), i.e. â‰¤3 frames â‰ˆ 45ms. A sustained 'crawl' below the gate is impossible: keeping the WMA-vs-EMA distance under 18px steady-state requires ~17px/frame (~1100px/s) continuous motion, which real saccades exceed (per-frame WMA deltas then exceed 18px and snap fires every frame) and fixation noise never approaches. So hunt item 3's worst case is bounded at ~45ms â€” but the header comment at line 3744 ('snap large moves (>18px)') and the Entry 9 changelog claim that filter behavior is 'byte-identical' are no longer accurate for steps in the 18-40px band, and Entry 10's '~zero added lag' holds only for steady-state tracking (0.8-sample ramp lag), not for small fixation steps. Worth either renormalizing the gate (compare gazeNow, not the WMA output, against prev) or updating the comment so future tuning isn't done against a wrong stated threshold.
